headline: Multi-agent systems and agent security dominate the quarter as browser sandboxing primitives break out alongside them.
body: Multi-agent collaboration runs through the window's strongest items, from Google Cloud's ADK and A2A game-building demo to Tencent WorkBuddy's 100-plus expert agents and Kagenti's identity and delegation-chain security layer for agentic deployments. The AI layer concept is weakening even at a high ratio of 145, while self-improvement, code navigation, Language Server Protocol, and AI-assisted development all lag. New entrants cluster on retrieval and structure (RAG, knowledge graphs, MCP, design systems) and on browser isolation primitives (iframe sandbox, same-origin policy, srcdoc attribute), all marked as breakouts. On the efficiency front, NVIDIA cut diffusion denoising from fifty steps toward one using quantization, latent caching, and distillation for near real-time video on Blackwell B200.

cited_items.title: Building with ADK & A2A: How multi-agent collaboration designs a game
You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
Tencent Workbuddy: RIP Claude,Codex! This is ACTUALLY quite a bit CRAZY!
